2603.25501.title: An Experimental Comparison of the Most Popular Approaches to Fake News Detection
2603.25501.paper_summary: In recent years, fake news detection has received increasing attention in public debate and scientific research. Despite advances in detection techniques, the production and spread of false information have become more sophisticated, driven by Large Language Models (LLMs) and the amplification power of social media. We present a critical assessment of 12 representative fake news detection approaches, spanning traditional machine learning, deep learning, transformers, and specialized cross-domain architectures. We evaluate these methods on 10 publicly available datasets differing in genre, source, topic, and labeling rationale. We address text-only English fake news detection as a binary classification task by harmonizing labels into "Real" and "Fake" to ensure a consistent evaluation protocol. We acknowledge that label semantics vary across datasets and that harmonization inevitably removes such semantic nuances. Each dataset is treated as a distinct domain. We conduct in-domain, multi-domain and cross-domain experiments to simulate real-world scenarios involving domain shift and out-of-distribution data. Fine-tuned models perform well in-domain but struggle to generalize. Cross-domain architectures can reduce this gap but are data-hungry, while LLMs offer a promising alternative through zero- and few-shot learning. Given inherent dataset confounds and possible pre-training exposure, results should be interpreted as robustness evaluations within this English, text-only protocol.
2603.25501.paper_summary_zh: 近年來，假新聞檢測在公共辯論和科學研究中受到了越來越多的關注。儘管檢測技術有所進步，假信息的產生和傳播變得更加複雜，這是由大型語言模型（LLMs）和社交媒體的放大效應所驅動的。我們對12種具有代表性的假新聞檢測方法進行了批判性評估，這些方法涵蓋了傳統機器學習、深度學習、Transformer和專門的跨領域架構。我們在10個公開可用的數據集上評估這些方法，這些數據集在類型、來源、主題和標記理由上各不相同。我們將僅限於文本的英語假新聞檢測視為一個二元分類任務，通過將標籤統一為“真實”和“假”來確保一致的評估協議。我們承認標籤語義在不同數據集之間有所不同，而統一標籤不可避免地會消除這些語義細微差別。每個數據集被視為一個獨特的領域。我們進行了域內、多域和跨域實驗，以模擬涉及領域轉移和分佈外數據的現實場景。經過微調的模型在域內表現良好，但在泛化方面表現不佳。跨域架構可以縮小這一差距，但對數據的需求很大，而LLMs則通過零樣本和少樣本學習提供了一個有前景的替代方案。考慮到固有的數據集混淆和可能的預訓練暴露，結果應被解釋為在這一英語文本專用協議內的穩健性評估。
2603.25501.author: Pietro Dell'Oglio et.al.
2603.25501.authors: Pietro Dell'Oglio, Alessandro Bondielli, Francesco Marcelloni, Lucia C. Passaro
